Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * Shrinehttp://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * Shrinehttp://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] ** [[Beijing]] ** [[Hokuto]] == External Links == * [http://www.kamikawajinja.com/ Kamikawa Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kamikawajinja.com Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/上川神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto shrines]] ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * [[Nippon Kaigi]] (The Kamikawa Conference of Hokkaido Headquarters is located within Kamikawa Shrinehttp://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kamikawajinja.com Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/上川神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto shrines]] ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kamikawajinja.com Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/上川神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto shrines]] ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * [[Ōkuninushi]]
[[Sukunabikona]]
[[Iwamura Michitoshi]]
Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[:en:Asahikawa Station|Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto shrines]] ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * [[Nippon Kaigi]] (The Kamikawa Conference of Hokkaido Headquarters is located within Kamikawa Shrinehttp://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] ** [[:en:Beijing|Beijing]] ** [[Hokuto]] == External Links == * [http://www.kamikawajinja.com/ Kamikawa Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kamikawajinja.com Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/上川神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|43.75236|142.36476|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahikawa]] [[Category:History of Asahikawa]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:1893 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[sn:Kamikawa Shrine]] [[en:Kamikawa Shrine]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = [[Amaterasu]]
[[Ōkuninushi]]
[[Sukunabikona]]
[[Iwamura Michitoshi]] | name = Kamikawa Shrine | image = [[File:KamikawaJinja.jpg|270px|Kamikawa Shrine]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|45|8.72|N|142|21|53.02|E}}}} | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43|45|8.72|N|142|21|53.02|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]]
[[Ōkuninushi]]
[[Sukunabikona]]
[[Iwamura Michitoshi]]
Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[Emperor Ōjin]] as [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[:en:Asahikawa Station|Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto shrines]] ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * [[Nippon Kaigi]] (The Kamikawa Conference of Hokkaido Headquarters is located within Kamikawa Shrinehttp://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] ** [[:en:Beijing|Beijing]] ** [[Hokuto]] == External Links == * [http://www.kamikawajinja.com/ Kamikawa Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kamikawajinja.com Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/上川神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|43.75236|142.36476|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Asahikawa]] [[Category:History of Asahikawa]] [[Category:1893 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[sn:Kamikawa Shrine]] [[en:Kamikawa Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kamikawa Shrine}} [[ja:上川神社]] [[mr:कामिकावा तीर्थ]] [[sv:Kamikawa-jinja]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = [[Amaterasu]]
[[Ōkuninushi]]
[[Sukunabikona]]
[[Iwamura Michitoshi]] | name = Kamikawa Shrine | image = [[File:KamikawaJinja.jpg|270px|Kamikawa Shrine]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|45|8.72|N|142|21|53.02|E}}}} | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43|45|8.72|N|142|21|53.02|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]]
[[Ōkuninushi]]
[[Sukunabikona]]
[[Iwamura Michitoshi]]
Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] as [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[Hokkaido]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[:en:Asahikawa Station|Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto shrines]] ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * [[Nippon Kaigi]] (The Kamikawa Conference of Hokkaido Headquarters is located within Kamikawa Shrinehttp://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] ** [[:en:Beijing|Beijing]] ** [[Hokuto]] == External Links == * [http://www.kamikawajinja.com/ Kamikawa Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kamikawajinja.com Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/上川神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|43.75236|142.36476|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]}} [[sn:Kamikawa Shrine]] [[en:Kamikawa Shrine]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = [[Amaterasu]]

Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|43|45|8.72|N|142|21|53.02|E}}}} | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43|45|8.72|N|142|21|53.02|E|display=title,inline}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]]
[[Ōkuninushi]]
[[Sukunabikona]]
[[Iwamura Michitoshi]]
Nine other deities | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}} established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[Toyoukebime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] as [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]] '''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine. The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮). == History == * [[July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival. == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[Toyouke-Ōmikami]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命) File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor == Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[JR Hokkaido]]'s [[:en:Asahikawa Station|Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto shrines]] ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * [[Nippon Kaigi]] (The Kamikawa Conference of Hokkaido Headquarters is located within Kamikawa Shrinehttp://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] ** [[:en:Beijing|Beijing]] ** [[:en:Hokuto|Hokuto]] == External Links == * [http://www.kamikawajinja.com/ Kamikawa Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.kamikawajinja.com Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/上川神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|43.75236|142.36476|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[Ōkuninushi]]
