<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamikawa Shrine]][[en:Kamikawa Shrine]][[ja:上川神社]][[mr:कामिकावा तीर्थ]][[sv:Kamikawa-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358960]] Wikidata name: Kamikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358960]] Wikidata name: Kamikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:July 21|July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] as [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * [[:en:July 15|July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[:en:Nabeshima Naomasa|Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s [[:en:Asahikawa Station|Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station

[[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[:en:Nabeshima Naomasa|Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s [[:en:Asahikawa Station|Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station

'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] as [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * [[:en:July 15|July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[:en:Nabeshima Naomasa|Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamikawa Shrine]][[en:Kamikawa Shrine]][[ja:上川神社]][[mr:कामिकावा तीर्थ]][[sv:Kamikawa-jinja]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamikawa Shrine]][[en:Kamikawa Shrine]][[ja:上川神社]][[mr:कामिकावा तीर्थ]][[sv:Kamikawa-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358960]] Wikidata name: Kamikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358960]] Wikidata name: Kamikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:July 21|July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] as [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * [[:en:July 15|July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[:en:Nabeshima Naomasa|Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s [[:en:Asahikawa Station|Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] (The Kamikawa Conference of Hokkaido Headquarters is located within Kamikawa Shrine<ref>http://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc</ref>.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] ** [[:en:Beijing|Beijing]] ** [[:en:Hokuto|Hokuto]]
== External Links == * [http://www.kamikawajinja.com/ Kamikawa Shrine Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}}

Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * [[:en:July 15|July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[:en:Nabeshima Naomasa|Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命)

[[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[:en:Nabeshima Naomasa|Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命)

{{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:July 21|July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] as [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * [[:en:July 15|July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[:en:Nabeshima Naomasa|Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamikawa Shrine]][[en:Kamikawa Shrine]][[ja:上川神社]][[mr:कामिकावा तीर्थ]][[sv:Kamikawa-jinja]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamikawa Shrine]][[en:Kamikawa Shrine]][[ja:上川神社]][[mr:कामिकावा तीर्थ]][[sv:Kamikawa-jinja]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamikawa Shrine]][[en:Kamikawa Shrine]][[ja:上川神社]][[mr:कामिकावा तीर्थ]][[sv:Kamikawa-jinja]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamikawa Shrine]][[en:Kamikawa Shrine]][[ja:上川神社]][[mr:कामिकावा तीर्थ]][[sv:Kamikawa-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358960]] Wikidata name: Kamikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358960]] Wikidata name: Kamikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:July 21|July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] as [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * [[:en:July 15|July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[:en:Nabeshima Naomasa|Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s [[:en:Asahikawa Station|Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station

== External Links == * [http://www.kamikawajinja.com/ Kamikawa Shrine Official Website]

Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).

[[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.

{{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:July 21|July 21]] | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] as [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * [[:en:July 15|July 15]], [[:en:1893|1893]] (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * [[:en:December 1902|December 1902]] (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * [[:en:January 26|January 26]], [[:en:1903|1903]] (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * [[:en:July 1904|July 1904]] (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * [[:en:November 26|November 26]], [[:en:1906|1906]] (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * [[:en:June 1|June 1]], [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * [[:en:November 14|November 14]], [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * [[:en:June 29|June 29]], [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * [[:en:December 18|December 18]], [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * [[:en:June 6|June 6]], [[:en:1924|1924]] (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * [[:en:July 1|July 1]], [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * [[:en:1992|1992]] (Heisei 4): [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]], a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]] (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** [[:en:Nabeshima Naomasa|Nabeshima Naomasa]] (鍋島直正) ** [[:en:Kuroda Kiyotaka|Kuroda Kiyotaka]] (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * [[:en:Iwamura Michitoshi|Iwamura Michitoshi]] (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s [[:en:Asahikawa Station|Asahikawa Station]] [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] (The Kamikawa Conference of Hokkaido Headquarters is located within Kamikawa Shrine<ref>http://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc</ref>.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] ** [[:en:Beijing|Beijing]] ** [[:en:Hokuto|Hokuto]]
== External Links == * [http://www.kamikawajinja.com/ Kamikawa Shrine Official Website]
{{Shinto-stub}}
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.kamikawajinja.com Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/上川神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing]

'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.

Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|WD=Q3042467|lt=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|WD=Q2282|lt=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|WD=Q16646814|lt=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|WD=Q2983|lt=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s {{ill|Asahikawa Station|en|Asahikawa Station|WD=Q1073617|lt=Asahikawa Station}} [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station

'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]

The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).

== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|WD=Q3042467|lt=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|WD=Q2282|lt=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|WD=Q16646814|lt=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|WD=Q2983|lt=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)

{{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893}} (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}} | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|WD=Q3042467|lt=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|WD=Q2282|lt=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|WD=Q16646814|lt=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|WD=Q2983|lt=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)

26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)

== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s {{ill|Asahikawa Station|en|Asahikawa Station|WD=Q1073617|lt=Asahikawa Station}} [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893}} (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}} | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|WD=Q3042467|lt=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|WD=Q2282|lt=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|WD=Q16646814|lt=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|WD=Q2983|lt=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s {{ill|Asahikawa Station|en|Asahikawa Station|WD=Q1073617|lt=Asahikawa Station}} [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station

Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893}} (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}} | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|WD=Q3042467|lt=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|WD=Q2282|lt=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|WD=Q16646814|lt=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|WD=Q2983|lt=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s {{ill|Asahikawa Station|en|Asahikawa Station|WD=Q1073617|lt=Asahikawa Station}} [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station
== References == {{Reflist}}

Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of == Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)
