{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21|1=July 21}} | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|1=Asahikawa}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|WD=Q3042467|lt=December 1902|1=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|WD=Q2282|lt=January 26|1=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|WD=Q16646814|lt=July 1904|1=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26|1=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906|1=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1|1=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|WD=Q2983|lt=November 14|1=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18|1=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6|1=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924|1=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa|1=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka|1=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s {{ill|Asahikawa Station|en|Asahikawa Station|WD=Q1073617|lt=Asahikawa Station|1=Asahikawa Station}} [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|en|Nippon Kaigi|WD=Q11507216|lt=Nippon Kaigi|1=Nippon Kaigi}} (The Kamikawa Conference of Hokkaido Headquarters is located within Kamikawa Shrine<ref>http://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc</ref>.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] ** {{ill|Beijing|en|Beijing|WD=Q956|lt=Beijing|1=Beijing}} ** {{ill|Hokuto|en|Hokuto|WD=Q298873|lt=Hokuto|1=Hokuto}}

* {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa|1=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka|1=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21|1=July 21}} | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|1=Asahikawa}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|WD=Q3042467|lt=December 1902|1=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|WD=Q2282|lt=January 26|1=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|WD=Q16646814|lt=July 1904|1=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26|1=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906|1=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1|1=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|WD=Q2983|lt=November 14|1=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18|1=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6|1=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924|1=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa|1=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka|1=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s {{ill|Asahikawa Station|en|Asahikawa Station|WD=Q1073617|lt=Asahikawa Station|1=Asahikawa Station}} [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} ** [[Shrine Ranking system]] * [[Tokiwakoen Park]] * {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|en|Nippon Kaigi|WD=Q11507216|lt=Nippon Kaigi|1=Nippon Kaigi}} (The Kamikawa Conference of Hokkaido Headquarters is located within Kamikawa Shrine<ref>http://nipponkaigihokkaido.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-87.html?fbclid=IwAR08Wje8NdvcUNvSaEez6JMQtphTHT43-F7hbu2LJmSN3ZoKEY3KT7q-1Rc</ref>.) * [[Kamikawa Detached Palace|Kamikawa Detached Palace Project]] ** {{ill|Beijing|en|Beijing|WD=Q956|lt=Beijing|1=Beijing}} ** {{ill|Hokuto|en|Hokuto|WD=Q298873|lt=Hokuto|1=Hokuto}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21|1=July 21}} | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|1=Asahikawa}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|WD=Q3042467|lt=December 1902|1=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|WD=Q2282|lt=January 26|1=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|WD=Q16646814|lt=July 1904|1=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26|1=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906|1=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1|1=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|WD=Q2983|lt=November 14|1=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18|1=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6|1=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924|1=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa|1=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka|1=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)

Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21|1=July 21}} | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|1=Asahikawa}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|WD=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|WD=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|WD=Q3042467|lt=December 1902|1=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|WD=Q2282|lt=January 26|1=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|WD=Q16646814|lt=July 1904|1=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|WD=Q3019|lt=November 26|1=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906|1=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|WD=Q2625|lt=June 1|1=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|WD=Q2983|lt=November 14|1=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|WD=Q2438|lt=December 18|1=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|WD=Q2620|lt=June 6|1=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924|1=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|WD=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|WD=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa|1=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|WD=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka|1=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|WD=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)
<gallery> File:Amaterasu cave crop.jpg|Amaterasu File:Emperor Ōjin.jpg|Emperor Ōjin File:Sugawara Michizane.jpg|Sugawara no Michizane File:Nabeshima Naomasa.jpg|Nabeshima Naomasa: First Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Kiyotaka Kuroda formal.jpg|Kuroda Kiyotaka: Third Commissioner of Hokkaido Development File:Michitoshi_Iwamura.jpg|Iwamura Michitoshi: First Hokkaido Governor File:Nagayama Takeshiroh.jpg|Nagayama Takeshiro: Second Hokkaido Governor </gallery>
== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s {{ill|Asahikawa Station|en|Asahikawa Station|WD=Q1073617|lt=Asahikawa Station|1=Asahikawa Station}} [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station

Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|qid=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|qid=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 6|qid=Q2620|lt=June 6|1=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|qid=Q2173|lt=1924|1=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|qid=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|qid=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{Ref improve|date=April 2024}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| |image=File:KamikawaJinja.jpg |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | name = Kamikawa Shrine | location = 2-1 Kaguraoka Park, {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|qid=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] | coordinates = {{Coord|43.752422|N|142.364728|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shrine Rank = [[Ken-sha]] (Prefectural Shrine) • [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] (Special Government Shrine) • [[Beppyo Jinja]] | founded = {{ill|1893|en|1893|qid=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26) | Main Building Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|qid=Q2727|lt=July 21|1=July 21}} | ceremony = Fire Drill ceremony ([[January 7]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=45|lat_sec=8.7|lon_deg=142|lon_min=21|lon_sec=53|label=Kamikawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | location = {{ill|Asahikawa|qid=Q200740|1=Asahikawa}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, ''Kamikawa jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Asahikawa, Hokkaido|Asahikawa, Hokkaido]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Japan Travel Guides, maps, Transit Search and Route Planner | website=Japan Travel| url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-1503649/ | access-date=11 April 2024}}</ref> established in 1883, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Amaterasu]] (天照皇大御神), [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神), [[Sukunabikona]] no Ōkami (少彦名大神), {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神), [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神), {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (天乃香久山神), [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神), {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命), and others. Its festival is on July 21. [[File:上川神社.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Kamikawa Shrine]]
'''Kamikawa Shrine''' (上川神社, Kamikawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|qid=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}}, [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a prefectural shrine.
The shrine has a sub-shrine called '''Asahikawa Tenmangu''' (旭川天満宮).
== History == * {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|qid=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}}, {{ill|1893|en|1893|qid=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26): The shrine was established in its current location, from Miyashita Street 4-chome to 7-chome in Asahikawa, as a guardian shrine for the pioneering protection of Kamikawa and Asahikawa. [[Amaterasu]] was enshrined as the deity. * [[July 1898]] (Meiji 31): The shrine was moved to 6-chome, 7-jo Street 8-chome in Asahikawa. * {{ill|December 1902|en|December 1902|qid=Q3042467|lt=December 1902|1=December 1902}} (Meiji 35): The shrine was moved to 21-chome, Miyashita Street, Asahikawa. * {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|qid=Q2282|lt=January 26|1=January 26}}, {{ill|1903|en|1903|qid=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} (Meiji 36): The shrine's establishment was officially approved. * {{ill|July 1904|en|July 1904|qid=Q16646814|lt=July 1904|1=July 1904}} (Meiji 37): [[Ōkuninushi]] and [[Sukunabikona]] were enshrined. * {{ill|November 26|en|November 26|qid=Q3019|lt=November 26|1=November 26}}, {{ill|1906|en|1906|qid=Q2049|lt=1906|1=1906}} (Meiji 39): The shrine was Ranked as a village shrine. * {{ill|June 1|en|June 1|qid=Q2625|lt=June 1|1=June 1}}, {{ill|1907|en|1907|qid=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40): The shrine was designated as a shrine receiving imperial offerings. * {{ill|November 14|en|November 14|qid=Q2983|lt=November 14|1=November 14}}, {{ill|1913|en|1913|qid=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}} (Taisho 2): The shrine was merged with the unranked Chikabumi Shrine. * {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|qid=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}, {{ill|1915|en|1915|qid=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taisho 4): The shrine was Ranked as a district shrine. * {{ill|December 18|en|December 18|qid=Q2438|lt=December 18|1=December 18}}, {{ill|1923|en|1923|qid=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} (Taisho 12): The shrine was elevated to a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|June 6|en|June 6|qid=Q2620|lt=June 6|1=June 6}}, {{ill|1924|en|1924|qid=Q2173|lt=1924|1=1924}} (Taisho 13): The shrine was moved to its current location, which was part of the imperial family's property during the Meiji era. * {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|qid=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}}, {{ill|1955|en|1955|qid=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}} (Showa 30): The shrine was designated as a special government shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. * {{ill|1966|en|1966|qid=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41): A branch of [[:en:Dazaifu Tenmangu|Dazaifu Tenmangu]] was enshrined within the precincts, establishing Asahikawa Tenmangu. * {{ill|1992|en|1992|qid=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} (Heisei 4): {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|qid=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}}, a significant figure in the pioneering of Kamikawa, was enshrined. * [[:en:July 1993|July 1993]] (Heisei 5): The shrine celebrated its centennial festival.
== Deities == * [[Amaterasu]] (天照大神) * [[Ōkuninushi]] (大己貴大神) * [[Sukunabikona]] (少彦名大神) * Deities from the merged Chikabumi Shrine: ** {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫神) ** [[Ōmononushi]] (大物主神) ** [[Amenohohi]] (天乃香久山神) ** [[Takeminakata]] (建御名方神) ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (譽田分命) ** [[Prince Atsuzane]] (敦実親王) ** {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|qid=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa|1=Nabeshima Naomasa}} (鍋島直正) ** {{ill|Kuroda Kiyotaka|en|Kuroda Kiyotaka|qid=Q714049|lt=Kuroda Kiyotaka|1=Kuroda Kiyotaka}} (黒田清隆) ** [[Nagayama Takeshiro]] (永山武四郎) * {{ill|Iwamura Michitoshi|en|Iwamura Michitoshi|qid=Q3156395|lt=Iwamura Michitoshi|1=Iwamura Michitoshi}} (岩村通俊) * deity of Asahikawa Tenmangu: ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (Sugawara no Michizane命)

== Access == * Approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:JR Hokkaido|JR Hokkaido]]'s {{ill|Asahikawa Station|en|Asahikawa Station|qid=Q1073617|lt=Asahikawa Station|1=Asahikawa Station}} [[File:上川神社バス停.JPG|thumb|Kamikawa Shrine bus stop]] * Approximately 5 minutes on foot from the Kamikawa Shrine bus stop, accessed via [[Asahikawa Electric Railway]] bus from the same station
